findings.issue: The draft over-generalizes differentiability for root functions.
findings.evidence: It says, “For polynomial and root functions it always did,” referring to the derivative limit existing. Root functions do not always have finite derivatives at every relevant point; for example, \(f(x)=\sqrt[3]{x}\) has an infinite difference-quotient limit at \(0\), not a finite derivative.
findings.suggestion: Qualify the sentence to refer only to the earlier examples and points where the derivative was actually computed, or remove the claim about root functions always having derivatives.

findings.where: Definition 2.3 body, post-definition paragraph, Caution, Remark 2.5
findings.issue: Several non-seed additions appear without their own expansion comments.
findings.evidence: Examples include the informal tangent-direction paragraph, the added discussion of failure modes such as a quotient blowing up, the env-caution packaging, and the Leibniz-notation remark.
findings.suggestion: Add appropriate `<!-- expansion:... -->` comments before these non-seed additions if the deterministic linter requires per-addition markers.

summary: One blocking math issue prevents convergence. The draft otherwise follows the seed order and includes the brief-mandated theorem/proof, |x| example, required figure content, caution, higher-derivative notation, and worked higher-derivative example.
